comment: 55yo male, 25 years occupational exposure as chrome-plating-line operator in a metal-finishing plant (hexavalent chromium Cr(VI) inhalation + dermal contact during electroplating bath operations). Hexavalent chromium is an IARC Group 1 carcinogen (Monograph 100C, 2012) — established lung-cancer endpoint, with paranasal-sinus + nasal cancer also elevated in heavily exposed cohorts. No current chromium-attributable malignancy. Engine routing pending RF-OCC-HEXAVALENT-CHROMIUM-PREVENTION authoring (v0.3 Wave I occupational backlog) — when that RF lands, expected fire branches: documented exposure + ≥10-years. Forward-compatible findings keys used following established occupational-RF pattern (occupational_*_documented + occupational_*_years_ge_10). Until RF lands, patient profile carries narrative findings only. Validates the v0.3 chrome-plating occupational pilot scaffolding.
